channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built a free tool to open Apple Pages/Numbers/Keynote files on Windows — 100% in-browser
item.description: How I built a free tool to open Apple iWork files on Windows (no backend) TL;DR: I built iWorkViewer — open and convert Apple .pages, .numbers, and .keynote files right in your browser. No Mac, no...

item.title: World Cup Teams Are in a Race for AI Dominance
item.description: This year, FIFA is providing an AI agent that any team can use. Is it enough to level the playing field or will future winners be determined by which team can afford the best tools?

item.title: AI Goes to War
item.description: On the evening of 26 February 2026, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei published a statement that would fracture the relationship between Silicon Valley and the Pentagon in ways not seen since the Vietnam...

item.title: Python vs PHP in 2026: An Honest Take for Developers Who Are Tired of Vague Answers
item.description: You've read the think-pieces. You've seen the Reddit wars. Here's the actual breakdown. Every few months, someone posts "Is PHP dead?" on a dev forum and watches 200 developers argue in the...

item.title: How to Build a RAG Knowledge Base from Any Documentation Site in 5 Minutes
item.description: The Problem You want to feed documentation into your RAG pipeline, but web scraping gives you a mess of navigation, sidebars, cookie banners, and broken formatting mixed with actual content. You...

item.title: Disney agrees to pay $50 million to YouTube TV and DirecTV subscribers
item.description: YouTube TV and DirecTV Stream customers may be eligible for a cash payout, after Disney agreed to pay $50 million to settle claims that it forced the services to increase their subscription...

item.title: Building a Production RAG Pipeline with LlamaIndex and Pinecone
item.description: Most teams that try RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) get it working in a weekend. Getting it to stay working at scale is the harder problem. According to a 2024 report on enterprise AI...

item.title: An eval is just a test that returns a value
item.description: apte is an async-first test framework for Python where your unit tests and your LLM evals run in the same engine, with the same fixtures. The whole design follows from one idea. An eval is just a...

item.title: Implementing Chat End-to-End Encryption (E2EE): A Technical Guide to X3DH and Double Ratchet
item.description: Claims of "security" are everywhere, but very few chat APIs actually walk the walk. Most offerings fall apart under a true zero-trust audit because they rely on basic encryption wrappers....

item.title: Why Your Salary Calculations Keep Crashing Your Browser
item.description: Why Your Salary Calculations Keep Crashing Your Browser We have all been there. You are staring at a spreadsheet, trying to figure out your take-home pay, or maybe you are a payroll manager...

item.title: The 124 best tech deals for day three of Prime Day
item.description: It’s day three of Prime Day, folks. We’re nearly 75 percent of the way through Amazon’s four-day sales event, and unsurprisingly, many deals are still sticking around. There have been all kinds of...

item.title: Why We Built a Messaging App That Looks Like a Calculator
item.description: A strange thing happened during one of our product discussions. We weren't talking about encryption. We weren't talking about servers. We weren't talking about messaging protocols. We were talking...

item.title: Building ADV Agent: What I Learned Building an AI-Powered Motorcycle Route Community After 3 Months of Riding
item.description: Building ADV Agent: What I Learned Building an AI-Powered Motorcycle Route Community After 3 Months of Riding Honestly, I thought building a motorcycle route community would be straightforward....

item.title: Is this solar e-bike a good idea or sophisticated e-waste?
item.description: I like the idea of a solar-powered electric bike, but I don't think anyone should buy the new Phosgo Go5 - not yet, anyway. This "world's first AI solar e-bike" promises to "eliminate range...

item.title: What Europe’s heat wave means for the power grid
item.description: It’s been hard to look away from headlines about the European heat wave this week. Temperatures are breaking records across the continent, and the weather is threatening lives, shutting down...

item.title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
item.description: IBM has built a new prototype chip with around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, which is twice the density of the company’s previous state-of-the-art technology...

item.title: British Police Built a Sprawling Crime-Prediction Machine. Some Results Couldn’t Be Trusted
item.description: As UK police embrace the AI revolution, a WIRED investigation reveals the messy inside story of one region’s experiment with predictive analytics.

item.title: New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis
item.description: A previously undocumented Rust-based macOS implant and information stealer has been found to embed a prompt injection payload designed to trick a malware analyst's artificial intelligence (AI)...

item.title: Facebook’s Creator Studio has been revived as an AI companion app
item.description: Meta is bringing back the Facebook Creator Studio page manager, now "reimagined" as a standalone AI companion app. The new app aims to make it easier for creators to connect with their audiences...

item.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
item.description: A new, stealthy backdoor named Mistic has been deployed as part of suspected financially motivated attacks aimed at multiple organizations spanning insurance, education, IT, and professional...

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access
item.description: An unknown threat actor exploited a recently disclosed high-severity security flaw impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN as a zero-day at least two months before it was publicly disclosed, according to...

item.title: How agents are transforming work
item.description: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: As ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet told TechCrunch in May, what China can currently buy are older-generation deep ultraviolet tools — gear first shipped about a decade ago — the same machines the...

item.title: The top tech Prime Day deals to shop on day two

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.

item.title: How to Opt Out of Google Search’s New AI Data Training Feature
item.description: Google’s Search history update stores media uploads from your interactions, like images used in reverse image searches, for training its AI models.

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its second day, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11...

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.description: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

item.title: The 16 best robot vacuum deals available during Prime Day
